steps.title: Find the contrast signal
steps.content: The sentence contrasts Vernet's “mythological and heroic subject matter” with Daumier's “decidedly mundane” focus, marked by the word “instead.” This signals that Daumier takes the opposite approach from Vernet.

steps.title: Choose the word for rejection
steps.content: Eschewing means deliberately avoiding or rejecting—the sense the sentence requires to show Daumier's deliberate rejection of the mythological approach. The other choices describe actions that don't match this: expounding elaborates on a topic, caricaturing exaggerates it mockingly, and appraising evaluates it. This matches choice A — A is correct.
